name: Laguna de la Nava Trail
name_original: Laguna de la Nava — circular por los miradores de la Zona especial de protección de aves
description: Laguna de la Nava Trail is a moderate 4.6-mile hike with 30 feet of elevation gain, taking under two hours through a natural area.
region_intro: Hiking around Fuentes De Nava offers routes through the flat, agricultural plains of Tierra de Campos. The landscape is characterized by open fields, historical canals like the Canal de Castilla, and significant wetlands such as Laguna de la Nava. Elevation changes are minimal, providing accessible trails for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Fuentes De Nava The most popular hiking route is Laguna de la Nava Trail, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the significant wetlands of Laguna de la Nava. Another top favourite among local hikers is Santuario Nuestra Señora de Carejas — circular desde Paredes de Nava, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This circular route passes by the Santuario Nuestra Señora de Carejas. Local hikers also love the Hermitages of Tierra de Campos from Frechilla, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through the agricultural plains of Tierra de Campos, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Fuentes De Nava is defined by open agricultural plains, historical canals, and wetland areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy hikes Fuentes De Nava. The routes in Fuentes De Nava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 3 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuentes De Nava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Fuentes De Nava?
A: There are over 4 hiking routes documented on komoot for the Fuentes De Nava area. These trails explore the flat, agricultural plains, historical canals, and significant wetlands of the region.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Fuentes De Nava offers several easy routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062479243/canal-de-castilla-por-el-ramal-de-campos-circular-por-los-palomares-de-tierra-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla por el Ramal de Campos — circular por los palomares de Tierra de Campos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, perfect for a relaxed walk along the historic canal.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect on hikes around Fuentes De Nava?
A: The landscape around Fuentes De Nava is characterized by the open, agricultural plains of Tierra de Campos. You'll encounter vast fields, historical canals like the Canal de Castilla, and important wetland areas such as Laguna de la Nava. Elevation changes are minimal, making for generally flat and accessible trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102737648/santuario-nuestra-senora-de-carejas-circular-desde-paredes-de-nava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Nuestra Señora de Carejas — circular desde Paredes de Nava&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular path that allows you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Fuentes De Nava?
A: Along the trails, you can explore the unique cultural and natural heritage of the region. Highlights include sections of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR-89 Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava&lt;/a&gt; wetlands, and various historical churches like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5794776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Salvador&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any short duration walks suitable for a quick outing?
A: Absolutely. For a shorter walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067271321/laguna-de-la-nava-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.6 miles (7.5 km) and can be completed in under two hours, offering a great way to experience the wetlands.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Fuentes De Nava?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars. Hikers often praise the tranquil, open landscapes, the historical elements like the Canal de Castilla, and the unique birdwatching opportunities around the wetlands.

Q: Is parking generally available near the trailheads in Fuentes De Nava?
A: Yes, for most routes starting from villages or points of interest in the Fuentes De Nava area, you can typically find parking in or near the starting locations. It's advisable to check specific route details for precise parking information if available.

Q: Are there any longer hiking routes for more experienced hikers?
A: While the terrain is generally flat, some routes offer longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102753980/hermitages-of-tierra-de-campos-from-frechilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitages of Tierra de Campos from Frechilla&lt;/a&gt;, at 8.4 miles (13.5 km), provides a longer exploration of the agricultural plains, taking around 3.5 hours to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fuentes De Nava?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Fuentes De Nava. The weather is milder, and the landscape offers beautiful colors. Summers can be hot and dry, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for walking, especially given the minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks to look out for?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of Laguna de la Nava and the Canal de Castilla, you can discover historical religious buildings. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5673762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Eulalia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5673812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de Frechilla&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable landmarks in the region.
tips:
This special zone for the protection of birds located to the south-west of Palencia is part of a project to recover a wetland that covered 5000ha (currently it barely reaches 400ha).
Drying of the wetland was done on purpose in the 1960s to open up new farmland.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and length of the Laguna de la Nava Trail?
faqs.answer: The Laguna de la Nava Trail is generally considered to have a medium to high difficulty. It covers a distance of approximately 18.8 to 19.2 kilometers and typically takes about 5.5 to 6.5 hours to complete. Hikers will experience an elevation gain of around 841 meters, reaching a highest point of 1,972 meters.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins on a comfortable, wide path, initially paved and well-marked. As you progress, it enters a spectacular glacial gorge with streams, waterfalls, and unique rock formations. You'll navigate a plain with large boulders, and later the trail narrows and becomes steeper, ascending through zigzag turns. The valley clearly shows its glacial origin with a 'U' shape.

faqs.question: Where does the Laguna de la Nava Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is well-marked with PR-AV-39 signposts. While specific parking details aren't provided, trails of this nature in the Sierra de Gredos typically have designated parking areas near their starting points. It's advisable to check local resources for the most precise trailhead access and parking information before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring is noted as the best season for this route, suitable for anyone with good physical condition. During summer, be aware of the lack of tree cover above 1,350 meters, which means direct sun exposure. In winter, the trail can be significantly more challenging due to ice accumulation, requiring specialized gear like snowshoes and crampons.

faqs.question: What notable natural features or landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a dramatic transition from comfortable paths to a breathtaking glacial gorge. You'll pass streams, waterfalls, and distinctive rock formations. The destination is the Laguna de la Nava itself, a small, idyllic lake nestled below 2,000 meters in a glacial cirque, guarded by prominent rock formations.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de la Nava Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed in the provided information. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash in protected natural spaces like the Sierra de Gredos to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check local park rules before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the La Nava y Campos de Palencia Natural Area?
faqs.answer: The Laguna de la Nava Trail is located within the Sierra de Gredos, which is part of a broader natural area. While many natural parks in Spain do not require permits for day hiking, it's always best to verify with the local authorities or the visitor center for the La Nava y Campos de Palencia Natural Area regarding any specific regulations, permits, or entrance fees that might apply to this particular route.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length, elevation, and varied terrain, you should pack essentials like sturdy hiking boots, appropriate layers of clothing for changing weather, and sufficient water. In summer, hats, sunscreen, and ample water are crucial due to sun exposure. If hiking in winter, snowshoes and crampons are necessary.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers increasingly spectacular views as it ascends through the glacial gorge. The 'U' shaped valley and the destination itself, Laguna de la Nava, provide stunning vistas of the surrounding Sierra de Gredos landscape and its unique rock formations.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its medium to high difficulty, significant distance (18.8-19.2 km), and considerable elevation gain (841m), this trail is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy outing. It's best suited for hikers with good physical condition and some experience.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: The provided information focuses on the trail itself and does not detail specific cafes, pubs, or accommodation directly along the route or at the trailhead. For such amenities, you would typically need to look in nearby towns or villages in the Sierra de Gredos region.
